CheckTitle: Microsoft Authenticator on companion applications is disabled
Description: The Microsoft Authenticator method should have **Authenticator Lite on companion applications** (**featureSettings.companionAppAllowedState**) set to **disabled**. Authenticator Lite embeds a subset of Authenticator functionality into companion apps such as Outlook mobile, expanding where approvals can occur.
Risk: Allowing **MFA** approvals from **companion applications** broadens the set of apps and devices that can approve sign-ins, increasing the attack surface for MFA fatigue and reducing the assurance provided by the dedicated Authenticator app.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center at https://entra.microsoft.com/
2. Go to **Protection** > **Authentication methods** > **Policies** > **Microsoft Authenticator**
3. Select **Configure**
4. Set **Microsoft Authenticator on companion applications** to **Disabled**
5. Click **Save**

Recommendation.Text: Disable Microsoft Authenticator on companion applications (Authenticator Lite) so that MFA approvals require the full Microsoft Authenticator app.
